name: coderunner
description: A secure local sandbox MCP server that executes LLM-generated code using Apple's native container technology on macOS. Includes two bundled SKILL.md files (image-crop-rotate, pdf-text-replace) with Python helper scripts. Primarily an MCP server with skill examples.

dangerous_patterns: install.sh runs 'sudo installer -pkg' to install Apple container tool system-wide
install.sh downloads unsigned pkg from GitHub releases via curl
server.py uses Playwright async API for browser automation (broad capability)
entrypoint.sh manages container lifecycle with system-level commands
Primary function is arbitrary code execution (by design, sandboxed)
findings_summary.notes: This is primarily an MCP server (not a pure agent skill) that intentionally executes arbitrary LLM-generated code in Apple containers. The sandboxing approach is sound and the project is from an organization account with Apache-2.0 license. However, the install.sh requires sudo and downloads/installs a pkg file — users must trust the download. The two bundled SKILL.md files (image-crop-rotate, pdf-text-replace) are benign utility skills with well-scoped Python scripts. Requires macOS with Apple Silicon only. Flagged for manual review due to sudo install requirement and system-level scope, not due to malicious intent.
